text.title: Sprint Goal Formula Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Sprint Goal Formula application MUST enforce, plus a terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Every sprint goal MUST contain three named parts: (a) outcome — verb + user/system change, (b) boundary — explicit in/out scope edge, (c) measure — verifiable signal. Any goal missing a part fails the planning gate.
rule.rationale: Scrum Guide 2020 §Sprint Goal — anchors Sprint Goal Formula rule `three-parts-required`.

rule.statement: The sprint goal MUST fit in one sentence ≤30 words. If it cannot, the sprint is targeting two goals and MUST be split or de-scoped.
rule.rationale: Compactness-as-recitability research — anchors Sprint Goal Formula rule `single-sentence-cap`.

rule.statement: Mid-sprint scope additions MUST be graded with the binary test: 'does this advance the outcome inside the boundary?'. 'Maybe / sort of' counts as no.
rule.rationale: Sprint goal field guides — anchors Sprint Goal Formula rule `binary-scope-filter`.

rule.statement: At sprint review, the measure portion MUST be evaluated pass or fail — no partial credit. A failed measure triggers a retro root-cause discussion as the first agenda item.
rule.rationale: Scrum review discipline — anchors Sprint Goal Formula rule `binary-review-grade`.

rule.statement: A team MUST NOT commit to two concurrent sprint goals. Two genuinely independent outcomes = over-allocation; PM MUST descope or escalate staffing before sprint start.
rule.rationale: Single-throat-to-choke practice — anchors Sprint Goal Formula rule `no-two-concurrent-goals`.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition or triggered skip, then route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
